markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Bound export evidence for index artifacts, rebuild/update events, manifests, retention, and reconstructability. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: AIReadyEvidenceExportBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: AIReadyEvidenceExportBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 7aed606c; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

adversarial_review_v2.note: Bounded manifest (handles + hashes, payload_bounded), reconstructable computed from bundle hash + trace binding, reuses canonical ai_ready_evidence_export@1 dialect; PG-proven reconstructable/unbound cases (pipeline_tests:317, ai_ready_export.rs:152-164).

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-141 requires a bounded AI-ready evidence export bridge for compiled context bundles + retrieval traces, reusing the canonical AI-ready export dialect with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and real (non-mock) proof. The implementation delivers a bounded projection manifest (handles+hashes, no payload copy), wires it into a real DB-backed API endpoint, and proves reconstructability through a real-PostgreSQL integration test that round-trips the bundle and trace.
findings: src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_retrieval/ai_ready_export.rs:53-89 defines AiReadyEvidenceManifest + build_evidence_manifest: a BOUNDED projection (payload_bounded=true, only bundle_id/content_hash + trace_ids + provenance, never full payloads), reusing the canonical 'ai_ready_evidence_export@1' dialect with provenance/retention/reconstructable fields per the folded WP-1-AIReady intent. Implements the MT's manifest/retention/provenance/reconstructability scope.

ai_ready_export.rs:84-88 computes reconstructable deterministically: requires non-empty bundle.context_hash AND every trace bound back to the bundle (trace.bundle_id == bundle.bundle_id). This is the reconstructability semantic the MT requires, derived from real authority-row fields, not a hardcoded true.

Bridge is wired into a real product surface, not orphaned: src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_retrieval.rs:286 (export_bundle_evidence GET endpoint) reads the bundle via db.get_knowledge_context_bundle and traces via db.list_knowledge_retrieval_traces_for_bundle (lines 276-285) from PostgreSQL, then builds the manifest and records a nav receipt. sub_rule_2 satisfied: manifest sources real PG authority rows.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests.rs:382 compiler_persists_bounded_bundle_and_drops_over_budget uses skip_if_no_pg!/MemoryFixture (real PostgreSQL harness), persists a compiled bundle + trace, reads them back from PG (fx.pg.db.get_knowledge_context_bundle, traces_for_bundle), then at lines 469-471 calls build_evidence_manifest on the round-tripped rows and asserts manifest.reconstructable && dialect=='ai_ready_evidence_export@1'. Proof touches the Handshake-owned PG-managed bundle/trace tables, not implementer-authored mocks.

Negative case present: ai_ready_export.rs:153-159 not_reconstructable_when_trace_unbound asserts reconstructable=false when a trace has no bundle binding, exercising the failure branch. The in-file tests (152-164) are in-memory unit cases but the durable-authority claim is independently covered by the real-PG pipeline test above.

No reachable placeholders/escapes: grep of impl + API for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/SQLite/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-' found only a doc-comment 'folded-stub requirement' reference and a positive 'no SQLite' assertion (api line 11). sub_rule_1 clean.

Spec alignment: §2.3.14 AI-Ready Data Architecture exists in current v02.194 (02-system-architecture.md:2730) and mandates provenance + hashes + retention fields (lines 589, 564); the impl reuses exactly that dialect as a bounded projection over PostgreSQL authority. No forbidden surfaces (no external search engine, no markdown-vault-as-authority, no SQLite). sub_rule_3: claimed_by/completed_by both null, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION — not a self-cert handoff.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): MT evidence string cites 'ai_ready_export.rs:152-164' for PG proof, but the actual real-PG proof lives in the pipeline test (line 469-471); the 152-164 cite points at the in-file unit tests. This is an imprecise evidence pointer, not a code defect — the required real-PG proof genuinely exists.
evidence_checked: git show 7aed606c (impl commit, full diff of ai_ready_export.rs)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
